name: vDisplay
summary: Enumerate windows and monitors (multi-backend, each with a natural-language description) and diagnose the display session through vdisplay:// routes — the environment-perception layer a resolve-first control plane composes.
description: vdisplay is a cross-platform virtual-display and window/monitor orchestration package. This connector exposes its programmatic enumeration as first-class vdisplay:// URI routes so a urirun node, flow, or readiness kernel can consume the environment state instead of re-deriving it ad hoc. Windows and monitors carry an nl field (a natural-language description of what they contain); window enumeration uses vdisplay's best available backend for the live session, so it can see application windows (including Chrome on GNOME-Wayland) where a single AT-SPI/X11 probe cannot. Read-only queries run in-process; vdisplay is imported lazily so the connector stays light.
useCases: Give a readiness kernel a trustworthy window list (unambiguous focus owner) before any input.
Resolve a window by title so a plan targets a specific window, not whatever holds focus.
List monitors/outputs with geometry and nl descriptions for capture/coordinate mapping.
Diagnose which enumeration backend is live so the planner knows if the window signal is reliable.
